label: Evidence Levels
description: Canonical hierarchy used for source prioritization, decay policies and conflict resolution.
usageNotes: This is ICICSO's operational hierarchy, not a verbatim import of any single external framework.
Lower `priority` means stronger default precedence in conflicts.

entries.label: Directive
entries.description: Normative evidence directly intended to guide practice or execution.
usageNotes: Includes guidelines, AUC, pathways, protocols and order sets when governed.

entries.label: Synthesis
entries.description: Systematic or quantitative synthesis of multiple eligible studies.
usageNotes: Generally prevails over isolated primary studies if current and applicable.

entries.label: Comparative Primary
entries.description: Primary comparative studies such as randomized or pragmatic trials.
usageNotes: Core source for effect estimates prior to synthesis.

entries.label: Observational
entries.description: Observational evidence including cohorts, case-control, registries and prognosis studies.
usageNotes: Important for harms, external validity and long-term follow-up.

entries.label: Contextual Implementation
entries.description: Evidence centered on implementation feasibility, economics or local adaptation.
usageNotes: Can constrain readiness but should not invert clinical truth.

entries.label: Background
entries.description: Background or low-rigor evidence that informs context but not direct execution.
usageNotes: Requires explicit governance if used to close evidence gaps.
